seasons.notes: Statewide archery season for both mule deer and white-tailed deer.
Youth-only deer season preceding the general opener.
Statewide general rifle season. General deer license required.
Statewide muzzleloader season following the general season.
Shoulder seasons available in select hunting districts before and after archery and general seasons. Check district-specific regulations.
Special backcountry season in Hunting Districts 150 and 250.

bagLimits.general: 1 deer per general deer license; additional Deer B tags available by drawing and over-the-counter
bagLimits.deerB: 1 antlerless deer per Deer B tag
bagLimits.notes: Check the Deer, Elk, and Antelope regulation booklet for district-specific limits. CWD check stations required in some areas.

licenseRequired: Deer B (optional, by drawing or OTC)

seasons.notes: Statewide archery elk season. General elk license required.
Statewide general rifle elk season. General elk license required.
Statewide muzzleloader elk season.
Elk shoulder seasons in select districts. Check district-specific regulations.
Special backcountry elk season in Hunting Districts 150 and 250.

bagLimits.general: 1 bull elk per general elk license; antlerless elk by Elk B tag only
bagLimits.elkB: 1 antlerless elk per Elk B tag (by drawing or OTC in select districts)
bagLimits.notes: Check the Deer, Elk, and Antelope regulation booklet for district-specific antler restrictions and antlerless opportunities.

licenseRequired: Elk B (optional, by drawing or OTC)

seasons.notes: 900 series licenses cover the full combined season window.
Archery-only antelope season.
General rifle antelope season. License required by special drawing.

bagLimits.general: 1 antelope per license; Antelope B tag available for antlerless in select districts
bagLimits.notes: Check the Deer, Elk, and Antelope regulation booklet for district-specific limits and antlerless opportunities.

seasons.displayDates: Apr. 15 – End dates vary by district
seasons.notes: Spring bear season. Dates vary by hunting district. Check current regulations.
Fall archery-only bear season.
Fall general (rifle) bear season.

bagLimits.notes: First-time buyers must complete the Black Bear Identification Test before purchasing a license. Check current regulations for district-specific quotas.

seasons.notes: Archery-only bighorn sheep season.
General rifle bighorn sheep season.
Winter bighorn sheep season in select districts.

bagLimits.general: 1 bighorn sheep per license (lifetime limit of 1 ram in some districts)
bagLimits.notes: Extremely limited tags. Nonresident quota is 10% of available tags. Odds of drawing are very low.

seasons.notes: Archery-only moose season.
General rifle moose season.
Winter moose season in select districts.

bagLimits.notes: Very limited tags. Nonresident quota is 10% of available tags. Odds of drawing are very low.

seasons.notes: Archery-only mountain goat season.
General rifle mountain goat season.
Winter mountain goat season in select districts.

bagLimits.notes: Extremely limited tags. Nonresident quota is 10% of available tags. Odds of drawing are very low.

seasons.notes: Archery-only mountain lion season.
Fall general mountain lion season.
Winter mountain lion season. Hound hunting permitted in some districts.

bagLimits.general: Varies by district; quota-based system with season closures when quotas are met
bagLimits.notes: Check current quotas and district closures before hunting. Restricted, Limited, and Unlimited license types available depending on district.

licenseRequired: Mountain Lion License (OTC or Drawing depending on district)
applicationDeadline: Aug. 1 (special licenses); OTC available for most districts

seasons.notes: Spring turkey season. Gobblers and bearded hens only. Check district-specific regulations.
Fall turkey season. Either-sex in most districts.

bagLimits.spring: 1 gobbler or bearded hen per spring license
bagLimits.fall: 1 turkey per fall license; some districts allow 2
bagLimits.notes: Check the Turkey regulation brochure for district-specific rules. Turkey regulations are typically posted in early March.

seasons.notes: Roosters only. Statewide season.

bagLimits.general: 3 roosters per day; 3 roosters in possession (3x daily limit)
bagLimits.notes: Hens are protected. Hunting is best in eastern Montana agricultural areas.

licenseRequired: Upland Game Bird License (or Sportsman License)

seasons.notes: Statewide season for ruffed, dusky (blue), and spruce grouse.

bagLimits.general: 3 per day in aggregate; 4x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.notes: All three mountain grouse species count toward the combined aggregate limit.

licenseRequired: Upland Game Bird License (or Sportsman License)

seasons.notes: Statewide season. Best hunting in eastern Montana prairies and grasslands.

bagLimits.general: 4 per day; 4x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.notes: Sharp-tailed grouse do not count toward the mountain grouse aggregate limit.

licenseRequired: Upland Game Bird License (or Sportsman License)

seasons.notes: Limited season in select districts in eastern Montana sagebrush country.

bagLimits.general: 2 per day; 2x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.notes: Season and districts subject to change based on population surveys. Check current regulations.

licenseRequired: Upland Game Bird License (or Sportsman License)

seasons.notes: Statewide season. Best hunting in agricultural areas of central and eastern Montana.

bagLimits.general: 8 per day in aggregate with other partridge species; 4x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.notes: Coveys can be large in good habitat years.

licenseRequired: Upland Game Bird License (or Sportsman License)

seasons.notes: Season dates vary by Pacific and Central Flyway zones. Check current federal and state regulations.

bagLimits.pacificFlyway: 7 per day combined ducks and mergansers; 3x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.centralFlyway: 6 per day combined; 3x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.notes: Species-specific restrictions apply (e.g., canvasback, redhead, pintail). Federal duck stamp required in addition to state license.

seasons.notes: Season dates vary by flyway zone. Check current federal and state regulations.
Light goose (snow geese, Ross's geese) conservation order with relaxed restrictions to manage overpopulation.

bagLimits.darkGeese: 5 per day (Pacific & Central Flyways combined); 3x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.lightGeese: 20 per day during conservation order; 3x daily limit in possession
bagLimits.notes: Federal duck stamp required. Electronic calls permitted during light goose conservation order.

seasons.notes: Season available in select zones. Check current regulations for open zones.

bagLimits.general: 1 or 2 per day depending on license type
bagLimits.notes: Application deadline June 1. Check zone-specific regulations.

seasons.notes: Archery-only wolf season.
General rifle wolf season.

seasons.displayDates: See regulations book for specific dates
seasons.notes: Wolf trapping season. See regulations for district-specific dates.

bagLimits.general: Quota-based; varies by hunting district. Season closes when quota is met.
bagLimits.notes: Wolf license is over-the-counter. First wolf license included with Sportsman license at $10; additional licenses $12.

seasons.notes: Spring bison season. Extremely limited tags.
Fall archery bison season.
Fall general bison season.

bagLimits.notes: Extremely limited tags. Application deadline May 1. $10 application fee.
